mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance depresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profitability expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5% for 2+ months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 100K persons month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite payroll declines, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising real consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with stable or declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals robust aggregate demand and healthy corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days in late-cycle expansion regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 665,071M and UNRATE at 3.4% as of 2023-04-01
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong consumer demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 45% of instances when both RSAFS growth > 0.5% and UNRATE < 3.8%, or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, which constrains corporate profitability and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% month-over-month for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances despite industrial production declines, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) serve as a leading indicator of construction demand and capital investment. A month-over-month decline in HOUST below 1,350K units signals weakening forward demand expectations and reduced economic momentum, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown or housing market contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when HOUST < 1,350K and declining > 3%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 3.0% year-over-year combined with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% signals the Federal Reserve maintaining restrictive policy to combat persistent inflation, which constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 302.845 and FEDFUNDS at 4.83% as of 2023-04-01
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure with persistent inflation and restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when both CPIAUCSL YoY > 3.0% and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) serves as a real-time labor market health indicator. When UNRATE rises above 3.8% and increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances despite UNRATE rising above 3.8% by > 0.2pp, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with rising retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust aggregate demand and economic expansion. When both indicators show positive momentum, equity valuations expand through higher earnings expectations and lower recession risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 1.5% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 22,439.607B and RSAFS at 665,071M as of 2023-01-01 and 2023-03-01
regime_hint: economic expansion with strong demand growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 45% of instances when both GDPC1 QoQ > 1.5% and RSAFS MoM > 0.3%, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year window.
